key_insights.insight: Gratitude is about perspective, not possessions; shifting one's perspective can lead to happiness regardless of circumstances.
key_insights.quote: It's actually all about my perspective on the things I have and my perspective on who I am, where I am and that's what I call slum gratitude.

key_insights.insight: Every time someone makes an excuse, they are disrespecting the privilege they were given at birth; use privilege as a 'lottery ticket' for achievement.
key_insights.quote: Every time you make an excuse, you are disrespecting the privilege you were given when you were born.

key_insights.insight: While 'hard work' is often criticised for promoting mental health issues, it serves as a great weapon and advantage in a competitive world, if you love what you do, it won't feel like hard work.
key_insights.quote: Hard work okay it's not just about hard work but as I said it serves as agreat advantage to get you ahead of competition to get you ahead of yourself and to push thingsforward faster and in a world that is so tremendously competitive in all arenas any advantage is a useful advantage I encourage hard work and I'm not ashamed of that.

key_insights.insight: Your social media timeline is your 'modern-day library' so curate it carefully to improve your mental state and information consumption.
key_insights.quote: Your timeline is your library. And if it is a library, what are the books that I'm currently reading?

key_insights.insight: A strong and well-maintained network is invaluable for business success, it can provide critical support, opportunities, and connections that can change the game.
key_insights.quote: There are so many tremendous entrepreneurs I've read about that have got to billions and billions of pounds in revenues just by one person, one client, one supplier that they managed to meet, your network can change the game.

key_insights.insight: People who proactively 'take' what they want by asking for more and believing in their ability to achieve it, get ahead in life.
key_insights.quote: People that get ahead and get more from life are the ones that ask for more... They end up getting what they take.

key_insights.insight: Instead of trying to 'get rich quick', the path to sustainable financial freedom lies in personal and self-development, and building wealth slowly.
key_insights.quote: You should aspire to get rich slowly instead of winning the lottery... Wealth is longevity. It's sustainable. It's rewarding. It's earned.

key_insights.insight: Developing both compassion and a controlled form of aggressiveness is important in business; however, it's vital to remain in control of one's emotions.
key_insights.quote: It's important to be the nice guy, but also to be the tough guy at the same time... So when I do become very direct or very aggressive or very stern, it can't be an emotional thing. It has to be an act.

key_insights.insight: Overcomplexity and constant connectivity negatively impact mental health; simplification, disconnection, and intentional activities like reading are key to taking back control.
key_insights.quote: We have created tools that are ripping apart the social fabric of how society works. People need to hard break from some of these tools and the things that you rely on.

key_insights.insight: Providing positive feedback, like a simple 'well done,' can have a significant and lasting impact on employee morale and perceived value.
key_insights.quote: Just that little nice work with that well done was having a tremendous impact on people and that is free just to say well done to the person you're working with is free and by all accounts it means a tremendous amount

actionable_advice.advice: Track your time for seven days to identify where you are spending most of your time.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use time-tracking apps or manually record activities in a diary to understand time allocation.

actionable_advice.advice: Upgrade your social media 'library' by unfollowing accounts that negatively impact your mental state.
actionable_advice.how_to: Audit your social media feeds and unfollow accounts that post negative, uninformative, or distracting content, replacing them with inspiring or educational accounts.

actionable_advice.advice: Create a network strategy to connect with people who can add value to your life and to whom you can offer value.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify specific individuals or groups you want to connect with, and make a conscious effort to engage with them on social media or in real life.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice 'controlled savage' by using both compassion and sternness as needed, focusing on control and not emotional reactivity.
actionable_advice.how_to: In conflict resolution, start with a compassionate approach but be prepared to be firm and direct when necessary, always remaining calm and collected.

actionable_advice.advice: Simplify your life and reduce complexity by disconnecting from technology on a regular basis.
actionable_advice.how_to: Turn off notifications, set aside time for offline activities like reading or walking in nature, and consider a weekly 'digital detox' by turning off your phone.

actionable_advice.advice: Adopt a 'well-done culture' by actively seeking out and acknowledging good work from team members.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create a feedback loop where leaders provide information on people doing great work, make sure to take a moment to tell those individuals that you heard great things and that you appreciate their efforts.

memorable_stories: The experience in the Mumbai slum, seeing extreme poverty and surprising happiness.
The story of the Sri Lankan driver taking him only to businesses that gave him commission, illustrating the importance of network in some cultures.
The story of predicting as a teen that he'd sit on the BBC Breakfast red sofa and later achieving it.
contrarian_claims: Complaining is subjective and relative to one's world; those in developed nations complain about little compared to those in extreme poverty.
The trend of negativity toward hard work is concerning because hard work can be a great advantage.
Hopefulness isn't as powerful as the belief that you're going to get what you want; belief is more effective.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E9: Social Media Is Destroying Our Society
